executive_summary: The Swedish Krona has staged a remarkable structural reversal: USD/SEK at 9.19 (rising = SEK weakening) sits –4.4% YoY and EUR/SEK at 10.82 trades –1.1% YTD, with the KIX trade-weighted index +7% YTD per Nordea — the best 12-month G10 performance against USD. The Riksbank held the repo rate at 1.75% on 7 May 2026 for a fifth consecutive meeting and retained its March 2026 rate path (1.77% Q4-26, 1.99% Q1-28, 2.17% terminal Q1-29) despite Operation Epic Fury's energy supply shock, because Sweden's April CPIF flash collapsed to 0.8% YoY versus the Riksbank's 1.5% forecast — a 70bp dovish surprise driven by the temporary food VAT cut from 12% to 6% effective April 2026 through end-2027. This places Sweden in a uniquely benign domestic position relative to peers: while Norges Bank surprise-hiked to 4.25% on the same day to combat NOK-imported inflation, the ECB held at 2.00% with money markets pricing 80% probability of a 25bp June hike, and Sweden alone has the disinflation tailwind plus AAA-rated fiscal headroom (debt/GDP 34.5%, defence spending climbing to 2.8%). The core tactical tension is binary and war-dependent: an Iran-US Hormuz ceasefire memorandum was delivered to Tehran on 6 May 2026 — execution would unlock a relief rally toward EUR/SEK 10.50, while war re-escalation would force the Riksbank into a stagflationary hike that would expose Sweden's variable-rate mortgage stock and reverse housing stabilisation. We are tactically Neutral SEK at conviction 2/5 (waiting for the binary catalyst to resolve) and strategically Bullish SEK at conviction 3/5, targeting EUR/SEK 10.50 and USD/SEK 9.00 over 3–6 months.

tactical.analysis: The Riksbank held the repo rate at 1.75% on 7 May 2026 for a fifth consecutive meeting and retained its March 2026 rate path projecting 1.77% Q4-26, 1.81% Q1-27, 1.99% Q1-28, and 2.17% Q1-29 terminal. This places SEK with the lowest carry in G10 except CHF and JPY: -188bp vs USD, -250bp vs NOK (Norges Bank surprise-hiked to 4.25% on the same day), -200bp vs GBP, -185bp vs AUD, +25bp vs EUR, and +175bp vs CHF. The 2Y SGB at 2.10% offers no compensation versus the 2Y Bund at ~2.10% — Sweden trades flat on the front end despite cleaner inflation, which markets read as appropriate given the structural growth gap (Riksbank forecasts 2.5% GDP for 2026 vs eurozone 0.8%). The May statement introduced explicit hike-bias language: 'if the war were to have major effects on the global economy and trigger a broad and persistent rise in inflation, [the Riksbank] could be forced to raise the policy rate.' Markets price Riksbank approximately 15-20bp above current by Dec 2026 — a ~60% probability of one 25bp hike, aligning closely with the Riksbank's own 1.77% Q4-26 forecast and converging on Handelsbanken's pricing of 'almost a full 25bp hike by December 2026.' The tactical setup is balanced: any hawkish Riksbank shift on 24 June (with new MPR) supports SEK by 30-50bp on EUR/SEK, while a dovish surprise driven by April CPIF undershoot risks SEK weakening 50-100bp. The carry-to-vol ratio is structurally negative (-25bp annualised carry vs USD divided by ~9% 1M implied vol = -0.28) — SEK longs lose money in trend-driven environments unless terminal SEK strength offsets. The dominant tactical channel is therefore not carry but the OIS path repricing into the 24 June Riksbank meeting and the 5 June ECB meeting (where 80% probability of a hike is priced).

strategic.analysis: On a 1-6 month horizon, the Riksbank's structural divergence from peers becomes increasingly SEK-supportive. While the ECB likely hikes 25bp on 5 June (80% probability per Polymarket and money markets) and potentially again to 2.50% by Q4 2026, the Fed remains on hold with a cut bias (3.625% mid, projected 3.375% by year-end), and Norges Bank has finished tightening at 4.25%, the Riksbank uniquely retains the option to hike from a low base if the war's stagflationary pressure persists. This optionality is asymmetric: the market prices Sweden at 1.77% Q4-26, but the path could compress to 2.00% if war-driven CPIF re-accelerates above 2% — generating 25bp of repricing on the front end (worth ~50-80bp of EUR/SEK strength via rate parity). Conversely, a continued CPIF undershoot (April flash 0.8%) only confirms current pricing, with limited downside. The 6-month differential trajectory is structurally narrowing against EUR (Riksbank holds while ECB hikes — narrows the SEK disadvantage), narrowing against USD (Fed cuts while Riksbank holds — Sweden's gap closes), and stable against NOK (both holding). Real yields have shifted decisively: with CPIF at 0.8% YoY and 2Y SGB at 2.10%, real yields are +130bp — among the highest in G10. SEB's December 2025 SEK Views and Nordea's strategist commentary both highlight Riksbank policy stability as the primary medium-term SEK tailwind. The carry-to-vol ratio improves over 6M as vol normalises post-war, lifting the structural attractiveness of SEK funding-driven trades.

tactical.analysis: Sweden has the most favourable inflation-growth mix in G10 right now, driven by a temporary but powerful disinflationary shock from the food VAT cut (12% to 6%) effective 1 April 2026 through 31 December 2027. April CPIF flash printed 0.8% YoY versus the Riksbank's March 2026 forecast of 1.5% — a 70bp dovish surprise that has dominated recent SEK price action. CPIF-ex-energy is at 1.1% YoY for March (lowest since July 2021), confirming underlying disinflation independent of the VAT-driven move. This contrasts sharply with NOK (CPI 3.6%, core 3.0%, forced Norges Bank to hike), EUR (HICP 2.6%, ECB likely hiking), and GBP (3.4%, BoE on hold) — Sweden alone has the disinflationary tailwind. Growth momentum is mixed: Q1 GDP flash on 29 April printed -0.2% QoQ (first contraction in a year, +1.6% YoY), but the Swedbank/Silf Manufacturing PMI surged to 57.2 in April (highest since February 2022) and the input price index hit 81.3 (highest since 2022 — direct evidence of war-driven cost pressure). The labour market remains soft (unemployment 8.7% seasonally adjusted, 9.7% raw), providing slack that supports continued disinflation. The May Services PMI cooled to 52.5 from 55.9 — modest deceleration but still expansionary. The combination of below-target inflation, expansionary manufacturing, and a hike-biased Riksbank creates the optimal SEK setup: the Riksbank does not need to hike (policy supports growth) but the option to hike (Q4 if needed) provides asymmetric SEK upside. This is the central pillar of the structural bull case.

strategic.analysis: Over 1-6 months, the inflation/growth mix becomes unambiguously SEK-supportive. The food VAT cut runs through end-2027, providing a sustained ~50-80bp drag on CPIF — meaning Sweden likely runs below its 2% target for the entire 2026-27 horizon. Capital Economics has explicitly argued that 'Sweden's inflation rate to fall sharply in 2026.' This creates structural divergence from the eurozone (HICP forecast 2.4% for 2026) and Norway (CPI sticky at ~3.5%), reinforcing Sweden's real yield advantage. Growth recovery is modest but steady: European Commission Autumn 2025 forecast Sweden GDP at 1.8% for 2026, and Riksbank March 2026 MPR forecast 2.5%. The German manufacturing PMI rose to 51.4 in April — improving but fragile, and Sweden's energy mix advantage (hydro/nuclear, <3% gas dependency) means Swedish manufacturing competitiveness is improving relative to German peers (Swedbank Manufacturing PMI 57.2 vs HCOB Germany 51.4). The strategic risk is asymmetric: a war re-escalation that drives Brent above $130/bbl could push Swedish input prices through the VAT-cut cushion and force Riksbank into a stagflationary hike — but this is a tail risk (estimated 20% probability). The base case is continued disinflation supporting SEK structural strength via real yields and Riksbank optionality.

VIX at 17.39 (close 6 May) sits below the 20.0 threshold for risk-off and well below the late-March VSTOXX peak of 34.78. S&P 500 at 7,365 closed above 7,300 for the first time on 6 May on Iran ceasefire memorandum optimism — +5.1% YTD. SEK's risk profile has been shifting from 'low-liquidity high-beta European growth proxy' to 'European AAA defensive trade' — partly because Sweden's structural fundamentals (current account 6.3%, AAA fiscal, energy mix) reward it as a safer European alternative when broader risk sentiment is uncertain. The German composite PMI fell into contraction at 48.3 — a clear risk-off signal for European-exposed currencies — but Sweden's manufacturing PMI at 57.2 differentiates SEK upward. Net tactical: the risk regime is broadly neutral for SEK, with the binary war catalyst dominating any cyclical risk signal. SEK's traditional risk-on beta has decoupled in the current cycle as the differentiation thesis dominates.

strategic.analysis: Over 1-6 months, the strategic risk regime tilts modestly SEK-positive on three channels. First, if the Iran-US conflict reaches a durable ceasefire (memorandum delivered 6 May, execution probability 50-55%), Brent collapses below $90, energy costs normalise, European manufacturing recovers, and SEK strengthens via the European industrial cycle correlation (-0.3 to -0.5 EUR/SEK vs European Manufacturing PMI per the currency brief). Second, even without ceasefire, Sweden's structural insulation (energy mix, AAA, current account surplus) positions SEK as the European 'safe-haven within risk-on' — the trade Nordea has called 'the return of the SEK.' Third, US-related risk events (election cycle, tariff threats, Fed credibility questions) increasingly drive capital rotation out of USD into structurally sound European AAA economies — Sweden is a primary beneficiary as global investors view the SEK undervaluation gap as the cleanest expression of 'long Europe, short US fiscal risk.' The strategic risk is a war re-escalation pushing global risk-off into a regime where SEK's low liquidity penalty re-asserts (similar to early March 2026) — but this is partially offset by the differentiation thesis having taken hold throughout 2025-26.

Foreign ownership of SGBs sits around 38% per the Riksbank's November 2025 Financial Stability Report — broadly stable. The Riksbank's FX reserve programme completed in 2024, removing the SEK-negative flow that had pressured the currency in 2023. Net tactical positioning: marginally long SEK among real money, modestly long among speculators, no clear contrarian setup. Neutral with confidence 50 is appropriate — neither a clear contrarian unwind risk nor a confirming long signal.

strategic.analysis: The strategic positioning case is supported by the structural rotation away from USD that has taken hold throughout 2025. Bloomberg reported in December 2025 that 'Swedish krona's best rally in decades looks set to run in 2026,' with bank consensus migrating to bullish SEK positions — JPMorgan, BofA, Nordea, SEB, and Handelsbanken all forecast continued SEK strength. Foreign portfolio inflows into Swedish bonds have stabilised, and equity ETF flows show modest accumulation. Crucially, real money positioning still has significant room to extend: pension funds and central bank reserve managers globally remain underweight SEK relative to AAA peer share, given Sweden's strong fundamentals. The Riksbank's full reserve build is complete, removing a structural SEK-negative; if anything, the next phase could include reserve rebalancing toward more SEK-denominated assets in EM/Asia. The strategic risk is that the marginal overweight positioning identified by SEB becomes a stretched overweight by Q3 — at which point a war re-escalation or unexpected dovish Riksbank pivot could trigger a positioning unwind. We monitor the next SEB SEK Views update (typically biannual) for this signal. Net strategic: positioning supports the SEK rally but is no longer the deeply oversold contrarian setup of 2024.

The 6M strategic horizon limits the impact of this scenario — housing reversals tend to develop over 6-12 months, making this more of a 2027 risk than a 2026 H2 risk.

tactical.analysis: Sweden's external balance is an unambiguous tailwind. Q3 2025 current account surplus of SEK 93.2bn equates to 6.3% of GDP on a 4-quarter rolling basis — among the strongest in G10. Q4 2025 saw a smaller SEK 78.1bn surplus (goods surplus 67.6bn, services deficit 27.6bn). Net international investment position is an SEK 3,824bn surplus per SCB Q3 2025 data. The Iran-US war's terms-of-trade impact on Sweden is uniquely benign: Sweden imports minimal natural gas (<3% of primary energy mix), with electricity ~70% hydro+nuclear and Sweden being a net electricity exporter to Germany, Denmark, and Poland. While TTF gas at €44.84/MWh is +25.9% YoY, the direct cost impact on Swedish manufacturing is far smaller than for German peers — visible in the manufacturing PMI gap (Swedbank 57.2 vs HCOB Germany 51.4). Brent at ~$100 is mildly negative for Sweden's oil import bill, but cushioned by SEK strength on USD-priced flows. The structural exporter base (Volvo, Ericsson, AstraZeneca, Spotify) continues to perform: Spotify hit 293 million Premium subscribers in Q1 2026 with 15.8% operating margins. strategic.analysis: The external balance is the strategic linchpin of the SEK bull case at 0.16 weight. Sweden's 6.3% current account surplus is structural, not cyclical — it reflects the persistent goods trade surplus from manufacturing (vehicles, machinery, telecom, pharma) plus growing services exports (tech, gaming, fintech). The Riksbank's Article IV documentation and IMF analysis both confirm this is sustainable on a 5-year horizon. Crucially, the 2025-26 trend has shifted toward services exports becoming a more meaningful contributor (Spotify, Klarna, Embracer/EA Stockholm, Mojang, King), reducing the historical cyclical dependency on European industrial demand. The energy mix advantage compounds strategically: as Europe undergoes the green transition, Sweden's existing low-carbon electricity (hydro/nuclear/wind) means lower transition costs and continued export competitiveness. NBSK pulp prices remain stable, supporting the forestry sector (SEK 185bn export value in 2024 per Swedish Forest Industries Federation). LKAB's high-grade iron ore is positioned for the European green steel cycle. The strategic risk is a deep European industrial recession (German composite PMI 48.3 is the warning signal) — but Sweden's services exposure provides partial insulation, and rearmament demand (Volvo military trucks, Saab Gripen) provides counter-cyclical offset. Bank consensus EUR/SEK 10.50 reflects partial pricing of this thesis; we see asymmetric upside to 10.30 if energy normalises and German manufacturing recovers.

tactical.analysis: The Riksbank introduced explicit hike-bias language in the 7 May 2026 statement for the first time since 2023: 'if the war were to have major effects on the global economy and trigger a broad and persistent rise in inflation, [the Riksbank] could be forced to raise the policy rate.' This is a meaningful shift from the May 2025-March 2026 period of pure neutral two-way guidance. Governor Erik Thedéen's 22 April 2026 speech 'Monetary policy challenges in war-related supply shocks' further articulated the framework: 'the risk has risen somewhat that inflation could be higher than we expected a few weeks ago. At the same time, our low inflation starting point means that we have some scope to try to get a clearer picture.' This is hawkish-leaning communication couched in patience — exactly the message that supports SEK because it tells markets the Riksbank is willing to defend SEK if war-driven inflation persists, but is in no rush to cut despite below-target CPIF. The named minutes structure (due ~14 May) will reveal the internal split: Per Jansson dovish, Thedéen/Bunge/Seim aligned on hold with Seim emphasising upside risk. Anna Breman's departure for the RBNZ Governorship (effective 1 December 2025) leaves Thedéen with the casting vote — modestly hawkish-skewed. The Riksbank's transparency (published rate path, named minutes) reduces uncertainty premium and supports SEK structurally.

strategic.analysis: On the strategic horizon, the Riksbank's institutional credibility is a compounding SEK-positive at the dominant 0.12 weight. The Riksbank is among the world's most transparent central banks (published rate path, named minutes attribution to individual board members), which delivers two strategic advantages: first, foreign investor confidence is high — flows into SGBs are stable at ~38% foreign ownership; second, when the Riksbank does shift its communication (as on 7 May), the market repricing is rapid and durable. Erik Thedéen's repeated speeches about Sweden's structural housing vulnerability ('high debt and short interest-rate fixation periods make Swedish households vulnerable') signal a Riksbank that is alert to housing risks but not in crisis-management mode. The 24 June 2026 meeting will deliver a fresh Monetary Policy Report — the first opportunity for a meaningful rate path revision since March, and the most likely catalyst for a hawkish shift if April final and May CPIF print below the Riksbank's 1.5% forecast. Bank consensus (Nordea, SEB, Handelsbanken, Capital Economics) all converge on the Riksbank holding through 2026 with potential Q1 2027 hike — meaning the Riksbank's communication is well-anchored. The strategic risk is a credibility shock if the housing market reverses and forces emergency cuts — but this is a Q4 2026 / 2027 risk, not the 1-6M base case.

tactical.analysis: The Iran-US war remains the dominant tactical event risk, with binary outcomes likely to resolve within the 2-4 week tactical window. The US delivered a 1-page memorandum to Tehran on 6 May 2026 via Pakistani mediators offering reopening of the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for a nuclear moratorium and sanctions relief. As of 7 May, Iran's Mohsen Rezaei (Expediency Council) signalled rebuff. Approximately 23,000 seafarers remain stranded across the Persian Gulf; 'Project Freedom' naval escort operation is paused. The 8 April ceasefire has been fragile with UAE attacks on 4 May breaking the calm and Brent intraday range $96-$114. For SEK specifically, the binary is asymmetric: a successful memorandum execution would deliver an immediate SEK relief rally (EUR/SEK toward 10.50, USD/SEK toward 9.00) as energy costs normalise and European manufacturing recovery accelerates. War re-escalation pushes Brent above $115 sustained, forces Riksbank into a stagflationary hike, exposes housing/mortgage stress, and triggers SEK low-liquidity sell-off (EUR/SEK toward 11.20, USD/SEK toward 9.55). Probability assessment: 50% memorandum executed, 30% continued status quo (volatile but no major escalation), 20% material escalation. Sweden-specific tactical events: 13 May April final CPIF, 14 May Riksbank named minutes, 5 June ECB, 17 June FOMC, 24 June Riksbank — the calendar is dense with binary outcomes.

strategic.analysis: The strategic event risk landscape is mixed for SEK. The Iran-US conflict will likely resolve over 1-6 months in either direction, and Sweden's exposure to either outcome is significant but not decisive. NATO membership (joined 7 March 2024) is a structural positive — defence spending is climbing to 2.8% of GDP in 2026 with cross-party agreement to reach 3.5% by 2032. Finance Minister Elisabeth Svantesson framed defence spending as 'ensuring our children and grandchildren don't have to learn to speak Russian.' The 13 September 2026 Swedish general election introduces political risk but is unlikely to be decisive for SEK — both major blocs (Kristersson minority government and the opposition Social Democrats) maintain pro-EU, pro-NATO consensus. Sweden's banks have material Baltic exposure (Swedbank, SEB ~10-15% of group profits historically), which is a tail risk if Russia-Ukraine escalates further. Late June 2026 NATO Summit will set the next defence spending trajectory. The European industrial recession risk remains elevated — German composite PMI at 48.3 — but is partially offset by Sweden's energy/services advantages. Net strategic: neutral with slight downside skew if war escalates and Baltic exposure becomes a concern, slight upside skew if European recovery accelerates and NATO defence spending boosts Swedish industrial demand.

tactical.analysis: Sweden's fiscal position is among the strongest in G10 and provides a clear structural SEK tailwind. Sovereign ratings: AAA (S&P, Fitch, DBRS), Aaa (Moody's), AAA (Scope) — all with stable outlooks. Debt/GDP at ~34.5% (2025) is among the lowest in G10, well below Sweden's 35% 'debt anchor' rule. The 2026 fiscal balance is projected at -2.4% of GDP per European Commission Autumn 2025 forecast — a modest deterioration from -1.7% in 2025, reflecting ~SEK 80bn budget reforms (tax cuts, defence boost, Ukraine support, food VAT cut). The 13 April 2026 Spring Budget added SEK 7.7bn additional measures and confirmed the food VAT cut from 12% to 6% (April 2026 to end-2027) — this is the disinflationary fiscal channel that supports SEK via reduced Riksbank tightening pressure. Defence spending is climbing aggressively: 2.4% (2025) → 2.8% (2026) → 3.1% (2028) → 3.5% (2032 target). The cross-party SEK 300bn loan facility for accelerated procurement adds counter-cyclical fiscal support. Net tactical: fiscal strength is unambiguously SEK-positive, providing rating support and capacity for emergency fiscal response if war escalation requires it.

strategic.analysis: The strategic fiscal advantage compounds at the higher 0.07 weight. Sweden's fiscal headroom (debt anchor at 35%, current ~34.5%) provides genuine policy capacity that EU peers (Germany, France, Italy) lack. As Germany finally activates fiscal expansion under the new Merz government — driving Bund yields higher and reducing Germany's 'safe-haven' premium — Sweden's relative fiscal position improves further. The SGB-Bund 10Y spread at -30bp reflects this dynamic: Sweden trades through Bund despite Germany's larger reserve currency status. Defence spending at 2.8% of GDP funds rearmament that benefits Swedish defence exporters (Saab Gripen, Volvo Defense, BAE Systems Bofors) — a counter-cyclical industrial offset. The food VAT cut is a multi-year fiscal commitment that anchors expectations of continued disinflation. The strategic risk is a housing market correction triggering bank bailouts (Swedbank, SEB Baltic exposure, Handelsbanken commercial real estate) — but Sweden's fiscal capacity to absorb such shock is among the highest in G10. Net strategic: AAA fiscal position is the second-strongest pillar of the SEK bull case after external balance, and is unlikely to deteriorate within the 6M horizon.

strategic.analysis: The strategic USD liquidity outlook is the most reinforcing pillar of the SEK bull case at 0.12 weight. The Fed's transition from QT to balance sheet expansion (RMP purchases of $40bn in Treasury bills) marks a fundamental regime shift — global USD liquidity is now expanding rather than contracting, which benefits all non-USD assets including SEK. The dollar smile framework places USD currently in the 'weak middle' zone — global growth stable, US fiscal risks elevated, no acute risk-off. This is the SEK-optimal regime per the currency brief: 'SEK's best environment is the goldilocks middle where global growth is stable, European manufacturing is expanding, and USD is broadly weakening.' The 6M outlook for the dollar smile depends on three factors: (1) Fed's response to potential war-driven inflation (could force Fed to hold instead of cut, modestly USD-supportive); (2) US election cycle developments (mid-year volatility); (3) European cycle recovery (if German industrial activity improves, SEK strengthens vs USD). Bank consensus expects DXY to decline modestly through 2026, with MUFG, Nordea, and SEB all forecasting USD/SEK below 9.00 by year-end. The strategic risk is a war-driven safe-haven USD rally — but the current pattern (war does NOT trigger USD bid) suggests this is a low-probability scenario.

tactical.rationale: The tactical bullish skew rests on a uniquely favourable Sweden-specific setup: the April CPIF flash undershoot to 0.8% YoY (vs Riksbank 1.5% forecast) created a 70bp dovish surprise that has not yet been fully priced. The food VAT cut delivers structural disinflation through end-2027, allowing the Riksbank to maintain hike-bias language while the global stagflation narrative pushes peers (NOK, ECB) toward tightening. The structural divergence — Norges Bank hiked to 4.25% (CPI 3.6%), ECB likely hikes 25bp on 5 June (HICP 2.6%), Sweden alone has below-target inflation — gives SEK the rare combination of policy stability AND credible upside optionality. The Riksbank's 7 May statement explicitly preserved the right to hike if war pressure persists, which is precisely the SEK-positive communication that supports the krona without requiring action. External balance (6.3% C/A surplus) and AAA fiscal position provide structural floors. The dominant tactical risk is the binary war catalyst: 50% probability the Hormuz memorandum executes (drives EUR/SEK toward 10.50, USD/SEK toward 9.00), 30% status quo, 20% material escalation (drives EUR/SEK toward 11.20). The market prices outcomes broadly fairly but the asymmetric upside is the memorandum execution scenario, where SEK's structural advantages compound. Path of least resistance: range-bound EUR/SEK 10.50-11.00 until the binary catalyst resolves around the 5 June ECB meeting. Key catalysts with dates: 13 May April final CPIF, 14 May Riksbank named minutes, 5 June ECB (80% prob 25bp hike), 24 June Riksbank with new MPR. Conviction drivers: structural Riksbank divergence, energy mix advantage, AAA fiscal headroom, post-peak USD theme. Actionability: moderately tradeable via short EUR/SEK at 10.82 with stop above 11.00 targeting 10.50 (R/R 1:1.5), or long USD/SEK 3M call spread as war hedge. Weighted score moderate enough to size positions modestly (0.5-1% risk) given binary catalyst risk. The combination of unique structural disinflation tailwind and binary catalyst pricing creates a setup where tactical conviction is appropriately capped at 2/5 — sufficient to express the directional view but not so high as to ignore tail risks.

risk_reward.base_case_payoff: Short EUR/SEK at 10.82 → 10.65 base case (-17 pips). Modest +0.16% return excluding negative carry of ~2bp/day = -28bp annualised on 3M position.

risk_reward.asymmetry_assessment: Mildly favourable — the binary war catalyst is roughly equally distributed but the structural setup tilts the consolidation phase toward SEK strength. Expected value positive but modest (~+0.5% over tactical window).

weighted_score.interpretation: Net score of +0.61 maps to high conviction bullish (3/5). Nine of ten factors bullish; only Event Risk neutral. The dominant strategic drivers are Policy & Rates (0.24, +0.144), External Balance (0.16, +0.112), Guidance & Credibility (0.12, +0.084), and USD Liquidity (0.12, +0.078) — these four factors alone contribute +0.418 of the +0.605 net score.
weighted_score.mapping_to_conviction: Strategic conviction 3/5 (rather than higher 4/5) despite +0.605 net score reflects the housing market vulnerability tail risk (~25% probability) and binary war catalyst — both of which prevent conviction from being elevated to 4/5. The strategic case is broad-based and structural rather than dependent on any single factor.

strategic.rationale: The strategic bull case for SEK is the strongest in our G10 universe currently, supported by the rare confluence of nine of ten factors aligning bullish. The Riksbank's structural divergence from peers (low CPIF, ECB tightening, Fed cutting, Sweden holding with hike-bias) creates a unique policy backdrop that compounds over months. The 6.3% current account surplus and AAA fiscal position provide structural floors — Sweden's external balance is among the strongest in G10. Energy mix advantage (hydro/nuclear, <3% gas dependency) shields Swedish manufacturing from European industrial recession risk — visible in Swedbank Manufacturing PMI 57.2 vs HCOB Germany 51.4. The food VAT cut delivers structural disinflation through end-2027, allowing Sweden to run below-target CPIF persistently — a unique disinflationary tailwind that no other G10 economy possesses. The post-peak USD theme reinforces SEK rotation as global investors seek European AAA expression. Bank consensus has converged on EUR/SEK 10.50 by year-end (Nordea, SEB, Handelsbanken, JPMorgan, BofA) — a 3% SEK strengthening priced. We see asymmetric upside to 10.30 (4.8% strengthening) if the war resolves favourably and German industrial recovery accelerates. The Riksbank's hike-bias is unpriced in current 1.77% Q4-26 OIS path — a credible Q4 hike could deliver an additional 50-80bp of EUR/SEK strength via rate parity. Path of least resistance: persistent SEK strengthening through 2026, with consolidation phases interrupted by binary war catalyst resolutions and central bank meeting outcomes. Key catalysts with dates: 5 June ECB hike, 24 June Riksbank with MPR, 17 June FOMC, 18 June Norges Bank, late June NATO Summit, ~13 September Swedish election. Conviction drivers: broad-based factor alignment (9/10), structural disinflation tailwind, AAA fiscal headroom, post-peak USD, Sweden's energy mix advantage. The dominant strategic risk is housing reversal — Erik Thedéen's repeated warnings about Sweden's variable-rate mortgage stock and 154% household DTI represent the credible bear case. Probability ~25%; would force Riksbank emergency cuts in 2027 (not 2026 horizon). Actionability: highly tradeable via short EUR/SEK targeting 10.50 with stop above 11.20 (R/R 1:2), or short USD/SEK targeting 9.00 with stop above 9.55 (R/R 1:1.4), or long SEK/JPY for carry-positive expression. The strategic synthesis represents the highest-conviction view on SEK in our G10+SGD universe currently and warrants meaningful position sizing.

g10_ranking.commentary: SEK is the cleanest expression of the post-peak USD theme combined with European AAA defensiveness. While NOK has higher carry (+250bp vs SEK), its 3.6% inflation forces continued tightening that creates fiscal/housing risks. Sweden uniquely has policy stability + credible upside optionality + structural disinflation — the rare trifecta in G10 currently.

EUR/SEK likely consolidates 10.50-10.95 through Q2 2026 ahead of the dense central bank calendar (5 June ECB, 24 June Riksbank, 17 June FOMC), then trends decisively lower in Q3 if the rate path repricing confirms Riksbank divergence. consensus_divergence.consensus_view: Bank consensus EUR/SEK 10.50 by end-2026 (Nordea, SEB, Handelsbanken, Bloomberg median). Riksbank holds at 1.75% through 2026 with possible Q1 2027 hike. USD/SEK 9.00-9.10 by mid-2026 (SEB, Nordea).
consensus_divergence.our_view: EUR/SEK 10.30-10.50 by end-2026 (slightly more bullish than consensus). Riksbank holds at 1.75% through Q3 2026 with possible Q4 2026 hike (3-6 months earlier than consensus). USD/SEK 8.80-9.00 by mid-2026.
consensus_divergence.divergence_magnitude: ~20bp more SEK strength on EUR/SEK; ~10bp more SEK strength on USD/SEK; one quarter earlier Riksbank hike timing.
consensus_divergence.why_we_differ: Consensus underprices the persistence of the food VAT cut's disinflationary impact (running through end-2027) and the resulting CPIF undershoot relative to Riksbank's own forecasts. Consensus does not yet fully price the asymmetric Riksbank hike-bias optionality introduced in the 7 May statement. Consensus underweights Sweden's energy-mix advantage as a structural differentiator versus German manufacturing in the war-driven environment.
consensus_divergence.payoff_if_right: Short EUR/SEK at 10.82 → 10.30 delivers +52 pips (+4.8%), R/R 1:1.4 vs stop at 11.20.
consensus_divergence.risk_if_wrong: Short EUR/SEK at 10.82 → 10.50 (consensus target) delivers +32 pips (+3.0%), R/R 1:0.85 — still positive but modest. Material risk only if war re-escalates beyond consensus or housing market reverses sharply.

cleanest_bearish_expression.rationale: If the SEK thesis breaks (war re-escalation, housing reversal, Riksbank dovish pivot), a clean technical break above 11.00 would signal regime change. This is the clearest invalidation expression with defined risk-reward.

relative_value_pairs.thesis: Norges Bank has finished tightening at 4.25% (CPI 3.6% peak); Sweden has hike-bias optionality. Mean reversion play — NOK/SEK has been elevated ~0.92 on the Norges Bank hike but Sweden's structural advantages will likely close the gap.

Carry-positive (+100bp annualised), expression of long European AAA defensive, short stagflationary funding currency. JPY remains structurally weak (BoJ at 0.75%, slow normalisation pace), while SEK has hike-bias optionality. Lower vol environment makes the carry trade attractive.
SEK provides European exposure with hike-bias optionality; CHF is stagflationary funding currency at 0.00%. Carry +175bp annualised. Both AAA credit but SEK has rate optionality CHF lacks.

cross_horizon_consistency.summary: Tactical (Neutral with bullish skew, 2/5) and Strategic (Bullish, 3/5) are directionally aligned. Both horizons see bullish factor preponderance.
cross_horizon_consistency.divergence_explanation: The tactical/strategic divergence in conviction reflects two factors: (1) the binary war catalyst is more decisive at tactical horizon (4-week window) than strategic (6-month window where multiple scenarios can play out); (2) the recent SEK rally (+7% YTD on KIX) has consumed significant near-term upside, while the structural drivers (current account, fiscal, energy mix, Riksbank divergence) compound over months.
cross_horizon_consistency.inflection_point: The 24 June Riksbank meeting with new MPR is the most likely inflection point — if it confirms hike-bias and revises the rate path higher, both tactical and strategic conviction increase. If it removes hike-bias, both decrease.
cross_horizon_consistency.catalyst_for_transition: 5 June ECB (likely hike), 24 June Riksbank MPR, Iran-US memorandum execution timeline, German Q2 industrial production data.
cross_horizon_consistency.trading_implication: Tactical positioning should be moderate (0.5-1% risk per trade) reflecting the binary catalyst risk, while strategic positioning can be larger (1-1.5% risk) reflecting higher conviction. The two horizons should be expressed simultaneously — tactical short EUR/SEK with tight stop (above 11.00) and strategic long SEK exposure via 6M EUR/SEK puts or buy-and-hold short EUR/SEK with wider stop (above 11.20).

forward_vs_spot.interpretation: Forwards price modest -0.4 to -0.7% annualised SEK strengthening over 3-6M, reflecting the small EUR-SEK rate differential (+25bp vs Sweden) and slight ECB hike expectations. Our base case implies 24 pips of edge on a 6M horizon (~2.3% return) — meaningful for institutional FX positioning. The total quantifiable edge supports active short EUR/SEK positioning rather than passive forward holding.

assumptions: CFTC SEK positioning is structurally unreliable due to chronically thin CME open interest. This dossier relies on the SEB SEK Views institutional survey (December 2025 vintage) and Riksbank Financial Stability Report data on foreign SGB ownership. A proper read on real-time positioning is not available from public sources.

1M/3M SEK risk reversals (EUR/SEK and USD/SEK) are not available from public sources at granular level. Implied vol estimates (7-10% 1M EUR/SEK) are based on cross-currency vol surface commentary and historical norms during war-stress periods. Risk reversal direction estimated from historical pattern (mild call premium for EUR/SEK).
SEK/USD 3M cross-currency basis is NOT FOUND from public sources. Stable Swedish bank covered bond spreads are used as a proxy for funding stress (no stress observed).

Valueguard HOX April 2026 specific reading is NOT FOUND from open Valueguard sources (paid index page only). Third-party context provides estimates: condos roughly -0.3% YoY and houses +0.2% YoY for full-year 2025.
KIX (Krona Index) latest specific point reading is NOT FOUND from open Riksbank API. Per Nordea analyst commentary, KIX is +7% YTD 2026 and at multi-year strong levels.
iTraxx Crossover/Main 7 May 2026 levels are NOT FOUND from primary sources. ECB Financial Stability Review November 2025 noted modest spread widening post-war.
Q4 2025 Swedish household DTI ratio is not yet published in open SCB sources. The latest authoritative point is 154% as of Q2 2025, down from 199% peak.
Riksbank rate path uses Q1 of each forecast year as horizons. End-2027 (Q4) and end-2028 (Q4) figures are interpolated from annual averages (1.9% and 2.1% respectively).
PPP table constructed using OECD purchasing power parity estimates and prevailing crosses. Estimates are directional rather than precise — actionable as structural undervaluation indicators only.

Iran-US war path is binary and rapidly evolving — sources cited date 4-7 May 2026. This dossier reflects the most recent open reporting available within the research window and may be invalidated by intraday developments.
